chat.title: Caregiver AI Assistant
chat.welcome: Hello, welcome to MedEase, how can I help you?
chat.patientDataPrompt: Sure! To retrieve a patient's data, please provide the patient's email and generated key in one message. For example: 'jane.doe@example.com KEY12345'
chat.reminderPrompt:
Sure! What would you like to be reminded about,
and when? (e.g. "Take meds at 9 AM for 5 days")

login.subtitle: Agentic AI that triages students to the right campus care — instantly.

errors.noConnection: Unable to reach the server. Check your connection and try again.

errors.accountDisabled: This account has been disabled. Please contact support.
errors.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment and try again.

hero.headline: Agentic AI for your
hero.subtitle: MedEase replaces post-injury panic with precision by intelligently triaging you to the correct care and campus services based on your university's specific data.

mission.heading: Our Mission
mission.subtitle: Infrastructure that transforms complex medical conditions into a navigable path.

cards.title: Closing the Information Gap
cards.text: The American medical system is a void for international students. MedEase acts as the bridge. We eliminate the critical asymmetry between injury and care, providing the infrastructure to navigate complex protocols with the safety and certainty of a local.

cards.title: Instant Clarity
cards.text: Stabilizing the student mindset by instantly shifting them from **Panic** to **Precision**.

cards.title: Preventing Harm
cards.text: Removing the fear of cost to shift students from **Hesitation** to immediate medical **Action**.

cards.title: Institutional Safety
cards.text: Shielding the university by shifting the risk profile from **Liability** to proactive **Compliance**.

sections.title: Agentic Triage Engine
sections.subtitle: RAG-powered routing that instantly connects students to the exact right campus resource based on live university data.

sections.title: Adaptive Chat Protocol
sections.subtitle: A living chat interface that adapts recovery timelines in real-time based on student feedback and natural language updates.

about.heading: Built from Experience
about.subtitle: We didn't just find a market gap. We lived it.

cards.label: 01 — The Spark
cards.title: The Moment Everything Broke
cards.body: During one of the most demanding seasons of college—juggling internships and classes—I suffered a Muay Thai injury that left me unable to walk. Like many students, I didn't take medical leave. I navigated stairs, long walks, and deadlines, all while recovering alone.
cards.highlight: I navigated one of the most complex systems in the world while I couldn't even walk.

cards.label: 02 — The Vision
cards.title: From Chaos to Infrastructure
cards.body: Recovery shouldn't be a solo mission. We built MedEase to replace that isolation with infrastructure. Our mission is simple: help every student heal with clarity, dignity, and compassion—turning a fragmented system into a seamless journey.
cards.highlight: We are building the digital safety net that I wish I had.

footer.tagline: The missing infrastructure for student health. Simplifying recovery, connecting caregivers, and reducing risk.

welcome.title: Welcome!
welcome.description: We're here as your companion to help you navigate your recovery journey.
disclaimers: This is not professional medical advice.
Always seek a qualified healthcare provider for urgent or serious concerns.
We cannot assume liability for decisions made based on this survey.

intro.title: Welcome to your personalized recovery journey!
intro.description: We'll ask a few optional questions so we can personalize recommendations, help you track your progress, and surface relevant campus resources.
intro.note: Everything is optional — you can skip anything that doesn't feel comfortable right now. We'll still support you.

questions.sectionDescription: Answer as much or as little as you'd like — this simply helps us understand where you're starting from.

whatHappened.title: What happened?
whatHappened.description: Briefly describe your injury or situation. You can keep it high level — this helps us anchor your journey.
whatHappened.placeholder: Optional: share anything you'd like us to know.

whenHappened.title: When did it happen?
whenHappened.description: This helps us understand where you might be in your recovery timeline.
whenHappened.detailLabel: If you want, choose a little more detail.

insurance.title: Do you have insurance?
insurance.description: This helps us think about budget-friendly options and which resources might fit best. You can skip this.
insurance.skipButton: Skip insurance and go to Questions Loop

end.title: You're all set.
end.description: Your data is safe with us. No worries — we're here to support your recovery journey.

questionsLoop.title: Questions Loop
questionsLoop.description: This section is separate from the initial survey. Think of it as your ongoing check-in space — ask follow-up questions, track symptoms, and get guidance whenever you need it.
questionsLoop.note: Next step: we can plug in a structured Q&A loop here while keeping the same landing-page visual language.

qitl.title: Questions in the Loop
qitl.description: Ask questions about your current care step.
qitl.placeholder: Ask a question about your care journey…
questions: How much does urgent care cost?
When should I take my medication?
What can I expect during recovery?
When do I need follow-up care?
When should I see a doctor again?

mockDiscussions: Has anyone had success with PT for runner's knee? Looking for Emory-area recommendations.
Yes! I went to the student health PT and they were great. Took about 3 weeks to feel better.
Seconding student health — and they can help with accommodation letters if you need reduced activity.

aiHealth.title: AI Health Assistant
aiHealth.subtitle: Get help writing emails, applications, and navigating health systems.
aiHealth.welcomeMessage: Hi! I can help you with accommodation letters, insurance emails, or navigating health systems. What do you need today?
aiHealth.placeholder: Ask for accommodation support, insurance help, or resources…
aiHealth.emailBody:
Dear [Disability Services / Student Access Office],

I am writing to request academic accommodations for the current semester. I have attached documentation from my healthcare provider.

I would appreciate the opportunity to meet and discuss an accommodation plan. Please let me know what steps I need to take next.

Thank you,
[Your name]

notes.title: Notes & Calendar
notes.description: Plan medical routines and review your care summaries in one place.

monthView.noteExplanation: Days with a dot have a saved consultation summary. Click a date to open that day's note.

dayNote.notesPlaceholder: Add personal notes about how you're feeling or follow-up plans…
dayNote.noSummary: No saved consultation summary for this day.
dayNote.noSummaryNote: Summaries appear here when you save a chat from the Home assistant.
